title: SpaceX Owns a Real Business That Makes Big Money
summary: A simple view of SpaceX is that it’s a low-cost rocket launcher that created the profitable Starlink satellite business and which is now burning cash to build orbital data centers and colonize Mars. Starlink doesn’t have the lead role in SpaceX’s $1.8 trillion initial public offering. But it is the real business inside the company, and the telecoms industry and its regulators can ill afford to be complacent about the disruptive threat it poses.

title: Cuba Poised for Biggest US Fuel Shipment Since Cold War Embargo

title: Morgan Stanley's Wilson Sees Big Shift From 60/40 Strategy
summary: Mike Wilson, Morgan Stanley's chief US equity strategist and CIO, says the recent equity and debt offerings signal a healthy market. Speaking with Matt Miller on "Bloomberg Open Interest," Wilson says there's enough capital out there to absorb all of the recent IPO activity. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bond Traders Keep Bets on a Fed Hike in 2026 After CPI Data
summary: Bond traders maintained bets that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates by the end of the year, even after a soft US core inflation reading eased pressure on Chairman Kevin Warsh to act sooner.

title: DCC Prepared to Accept Improved £5.7 Billion KKR Buyout Bid
summary: DCC Plc said it was prepared to accept an improved offer from buyout firms KKR & Co. and Energy Capital Partners, which valued the firm at about £5.7 billion ($7.6 billion).

title: Amazon Inks $17.5 Billion Loan in Financing Led by Citigroup
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has inked a $17.5 billion loan from a group of banks led by Citigroup Inc., the tech company said in a regulatory filing.

title: The Rich Are Getting Richer: What Do They Want From Wealth Managers?
summary: Merryn Somerset Webb sits down with Capgemini's Gareth Wilson to discuss the latest World Wealth Report, the rise of robo-advisors and family offices, and why traditional wealth management firms are facing growing competition. They also debate the question: Do investors want empathy from their wealth managers, or simply better returns? (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks Fall as US-Iran Tensions Offset CPI Relief: Markets Wrap
summary: A relatively tame inflation report brought a degree of relief to Wall Street, but failed to lift stocks amid heightened geopolitical tensions that are keeping energy costs elevated and threatening further price pressures.

title: US Inflation Picks Up to Three-Year High, Eroding Paychecks
summary: US inflation accelerated in May to the fastest pace in more than three years as the Iran war pushed up energy prices, outstripping Americans’ pay gains.

title: Man Accused of Igniting Massive Los Angeles Fire Goes on Trial
summary: The man accused of “maliciously” starting the most destructive wildfire in Los Angeles history last year is set to face a jury over arson charges.

title: Metals Drop on Renewed Middle East Tensions, US Rate Outlook
summary: Metals from aluminum to zinc dropped after tensions in the Middle East escalated and expectations for US rate hikes weighed on the demand outlook for commodities.

title: Oracle Earnings Pose Next Test for Suddenly Shaky AI Stock Rally
summary: The rally in artificial intelligence-related stocks is suddenly on shaky footing, and Oracle Corp.’s earnings report after the close represents the group’s next test.

title: SpaceX IPO Draws Billions in Orders From Middle East Funds
summary: Gulf wealth funds have put in orders for shares worth several billions of dollars in SpaceX’s initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, the latest indication of the region’s ambitions to continue playing a lead role in bankrolling the global artificial intelligence buildout.

title: Gold Slumps as Faster US Inflation Fuels Fed Rate Hike Fears
summary: Gold and other precious metals held big losses after headline US consumer prices rose by the most in more than three years, fueling ongoing worries that the Federal Reserve will soon need to start raising interest rates.

title: Lenovo on Strategy and Growth
summary: Winston Cheng, Senior Vice President & Chief Financial Officer, Lenovo said the AI infrastructure boom is probably “the greatest infrastructure build in the history of mankind,” when discussing company outlook with Bloomberg’s Sangmi Cha at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Sweden’s Top Pension Gatekeeper Wants to Keep Private Credit Out
summary: The top official deciding how Swedes invest much of their pension savings said he’s against offering them access to private markets funds.

title: US Inflation Accelerates in May, While Core CPI Softens
summary: US inflation accelerated in May with the Iran war pushing up energy prices, as the consumer price index climbed 4.2% from a year earlier, the most since early 2023. The core CPI, which excludes food and energy, increased 0.2% from April and 2.9% from a year earlier. Michael McKee breaks down the numbers on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Argentine Peso Drops Most Over Eight Weeks Since Midterm Selloff
summary: An incipient decline in the Argentine peso is gaining momentum, as investors grow more risk averse and policymakers in Buenos Aires loosen their grip on the volatile currency.

title: CIBC to Offer SpaceX Access Through Canadian Depositary Receipt
summary: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce is set to offer Canadian investors access to SpaceX through a vehicle that gives local investors access to global stocks.

title: SpaceX IPO Is a 'White Knuckle' Moment, Ives Says
summary: Dan Ives of Wedbush Securities talks about the upcoming SpaceX IPO and how it could impact tech stocks. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Anthropic considera peligroso a Mythos. ¿Por qué amplía su acceso?
summary: ¿Qué tan peligrosa debe ser una inteligencia artificial para que su creador se niegue a lanzarla al público, pero al mismo tiempo amplíe su acceso? Anthropic asegura que su modelo Mythos puede descubrir vulnerabilidades críticas y facilitar ciberataques sofisticados. Entonces, ¿por qué cada vez más organizaciones pueden usarlo? (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Wall Street Braces for SpaceX With Stress Test, ‘Watch Parties’
summary: Wall Street has spent months debating how much SpaceX is worth. Behind the scenes, a different challenge has occupied the institutions responsible for bringing it public: preparing the plumbing systems needed to support what could become the largest IPO in history.

title: EU Budget Leeway for Meloni and Peers Gets Slammed by Watchdog
summary: The European Commission shouldn’t have given in to pressure from governments seeking extra leeway for energy-related aid, the European Union’s budget watchdog said.

title: Apollo’s Kleinman Says Private Equity Lost Its Way on Deals
summary: Private equity “lost its way a little bit” during the easy-money era and firms will “have to start capitulating for sure on valuations” after borrowing costs normalized, warned Scott Kleinman, co-president of Apollo Global Management Inc.

title: US Premarket Movers for June 10, 2026
summary: S&P 500 Index futures fall 1.1% as of 7:45 a.m. in New York as weakness in technology continues and the US and Iran exchanged strikes overnight.

title: Banks Pay 7% on Dollar Deposits as India Seeks Fresh Foreign Currency
summary: Indian lenders have sharply raised rates on foreign-currency deposits to lure the nation’s overseas residents, offering an early test of the central bank’s recent measures to bring in capital flows to support the rupee.

title: Iraq Boosts Oil Exports as More Tankers Transit Strait of Hormuz
summary: Iraq is accelerating oil loadings at its main port and boosting shipments out of the Persian Gulf in the latest sign that the region’s top OPEC producers are getting more barrels through the vital Strait of Hormuz.

title: MTN Finalizing Nigeria, Uganda Fintech Spinoffs; Adds Alipay
summary: MTN Group Ltd. is finalizing the spinoffs of its Nigerian and Ugandan financial-technology operations as part of a reorganization of its mobile-money unit to allow Mastercard Inc. and other strategic buyers to take minority stakes in its fintech businesses.
